topics: Introduction to analysis of deformable bodies. Types of external loads -Normal
Bending and Bearing stress- Linear and Shear strains. Hooke’s law - Stress-Strain diagrams - concepts of Isotropy
Bulk Modulus and Rigidity Modulus. Poisson’s ratio - Relationship between elastic constants. Deformation in axially loaded bars - uniform cross section
principle of superposition. Thermal effects - simple
torsion theory of elastic circular bars
shaft design for torsional load. Beams- Classification

Diagrammatic conventions for supports and loading Differential equations between load, Shear Force and Bending Moment- Shear Force and Bending Moment Diagrams of Cantilever and Simply supported beam with Point load/UDL. Point of Inflection
Flexure formula for beams -assumptions and limitations
derivation and problems for rectangular section only -assumptions and limitations Deflection of Beams, Moment-Curvature relation
Stress on an inclined plane due to Uniaxial stress
Stress on an inclined plane due to Biaxial stress
Stress on an inclined plane due to two Normal Stresses accompanied by Shear stresses
principal planes and stresses. Mohr’s circle of stress. Buckling and stability of long columns
Euler’s buckling/crippling load for columns with different end conditions
Euler equation derivation for both ends hinged only
